approval_notes: Owner-approved F004 plan. Slices 1 (safe fetch guard + SourceAdapter contract, PR #14) and 2 (migration 0004 + run_scan orchestration, PR #15) are merged to main. This contract governs Slice 3 ONLY.

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a runtime/npm dependency, start Slice 4, or touch the offer_version immutability trigger.
F004 stays passes:false after this slice. A fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation reviews Slice 3 before Slice 4 proceeds.
objective: Add reconciliation to the ingestion pipeline as a separate pass (apps/api/app/ingest/reconcile.py) over candidates persisted by run_scan. It diffs a freshly-scanned candidate against the last-known candidate for the same source/identity and emits a DRAFT change_event with a deterministic change_type (added/modified/withdrawn/restored) and materiality (material/non_material/unknown); flags candidates/sources stale when source freshness exceeds the configured schedule/policy window (stale never counts as a fresh verification); and raises a pending review_item (evidence_conflict, recommended_action, admin_disposition='pending') when official sources contradict each other on a known material fact. Nothing is auto-resolved and there is NO publication path: reconciliation never creates or mutates offer/offer_version and the offer_version immutability trigger is untouched. All diff/materiality/staleness/contradiction rules are pure and unit-testable; reconciliation operates on candidate content only.
scope: migrations/versions/0005_change_event_candidate_link.py: ALTER change_event -> add previous_candidate_id + new_candidate_id FKs to candidate (ondelete SET NULL), make offer_id nullable, add CHECK ck_change_event_change_link_target (offer_id OR previous_candidate_id OR new_candidate_id NOT NULL). Reversible upgrade/downgrade. offer_version + its immutability trigger untouched.
apps/api/app/models/domain.py: update ChangeEvent ORM (offer_id nullable, add previous_candidate_id/new_candidate_id FKs + relationships, add the link-target CHECK).
apps/api/app/ingest/reconcile.py: pure diff/materiality/staleness/contradiction functions + reconcile_scan(scan_run, source, session, *, now) persistence orchestrator.
apps/api/app/ingest/__init__.py: export reconcile_scan (and the pure helpers/result types).

tests/integration/test_ingest_reconcile.py (live Postgres): end-to-end unchanged/changed/stale/contradictory; asserts draft change_event + pending review_item rows and that NO offer_version is created.
docs/DATA_MODEL.md: document the change_event candidate linkage and the reconciliation pass.
out_of_scope: Creating or mutating offer/offer_version (no publication path). The offer_version immutability trigger is untouched.
Auto-resolution or auto-publication of any change_event or review_item.
Wiring reconciliation into run_scan (kept separate so Slice 2's zero-change_event-on-scan invariant holds).
Candidate -> service/offer DB matching beyond the minimal content identity needed to diff/compare.
Residual R1 (DNS-rebinding IP pinning) from Slice 1 - explicitly deferred.
Any new runtime or npm dependency (requirements-sync guard stays green). Marking F004 passes:true. Advancing to Slice 4.
acceptance_criteria: Unchanged: reconciling a re-scan of byte-identical candidate content produces NO change_event and NO review_item.
Changed: a candidate whose material facts changed produces exactly one DRAFT change_event with change_type='modified' and materiality='material'; a brand-new candidate produces change_type='added'.

Stale: when the source's freshest snapshot age exceeds the schedule/policy window, the affected candidates are flagged verification_state='stale' and are NOT counted as a fresh verification (never 'verified').

Contradictory: when two official sources present different known values for the same material fact of the same identity, exactly one pending review_item (admin_disposition='pending', evidence_conflict populated, recommended_action set) is raised and nothing is auto-resolved or published.
reconcile_scan NEVER creates or mutates offer or offer_version rows, and the offer_version immutability trigger remains installed and intact.
Migration 0005 is reversible: upgrade -> downgrade -> upgrade leaves no model/migration drift (compare_metadata == []); the 0001-0004 tables + immutability trigger survive intact.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit pass offline (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit) including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency).
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict for Slice 3 before any status change; F004 remains passes:false.

risks.data_migration: 0005 only ALTERs change_event (adds two nullable candidate FKs, relaxes offer_id to nullable, adds a link-target CHECK). Reversible. offer_version and its immutability trigger are untouched. Relaxing offer_id does not weaken published history: existing offer-linked change events keep their offer_id; the CHECK still requires at least one linkage target.
risks.security_privacy: Reconciliation reads candidate content only; no credentials, no network. Contradictions raise a review_item for a human; nothing is auto-resolved. detect-secrets stays green.
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python + SQLAlchemy/Alembic already in use; no new dependency; amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: No offer data is published; change events are DRAFT and candidates are never verified; stale data never counts as a fresh verification; no Z0/free claim is asserted.
risks.regression: run_scan is unchanged, so Slice 2's zero-change_event-on-scan invariant still holds. Reconciliation is a distinct, explicitly-invoked pass.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm dependency.

notes: Owner approved the F004 plan and directed Slice 3 implementation, then stop for a fresh-context Level 2 slice evaluation before Slice 4.
